summary:
• Cuba's severe diesel and fuel oil shortages causing widespread blackouts represent a critical supply vulnerability in the Caribbean that could disrupt regional energy stability and force increased reliance on costly spot market purchases.

• Iran's case-by-case approach to Strait of Hormuz passage and ongoing crackdowns create unpredictable geopolitical friction in the world's most critical oil chokepoint, threatening consistent crude flows to Asia-Pacific markets.

• Major oil companies are redirecting investment toward previously unattractive exploration destinations (Egypt, Senegal, Mediterranean) due to supply tightness, signaling that traditional low-cost producers are becoming insufficient to meet global demand.
